action.reasoning: Observation: the DINOv2 attempt failed immediately because timm tried to fetch weights from Hugging Face and they are not cached offline in this environment. Analysis: the current best hidden-test score remains 0.564 from checkpoints/aug_tta.pt, and recent exploratory changes have all underperformed it. With 20 iterations and ~1.8h left, the highest-EV move is to avoid risky long retraining and instead do a controlled, offline-safe improvement on the proven CLIP backbone. A likely gain comes from ensembling the two strongest non-DANN models at inference (aug_tta + baseline), which can improve balanced accuracy by smoothing class-specific errors without more training. To support this, predict.py should accept one or multiple checkpoints and average logits. I will patch model.py to be explicitly offline-safe for the known CLIP backbone, patch predict.py for checkpoint ensembling while preserving single-checkpoint compatibility, run a local smoke test on val, and submit the ensemble using the best two checkpoints.
